title: Rich Australian Cornmeal Hash
description: A hearty, jammy, molten Australian dish featuring fine yellow cornmeal, perfect for curing hangovers, and a great example of wok-hei, made in a copper mug and jain-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the copper mug over medium heat on your stovetop.
Add the vegetable oil to the copper mug and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
Add the diced onion to the copper mug and cook until it is translucent.
Add the diced bell pepper to the copper mug and cook until it is tender.
Add the diced tomato to the copper mug and cook until it is soft.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the fine yellow cornmeal, water, salt, and smoked paprika.
Add the cornmeal mixture to the copper mug and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Use a spoon to create 2-3 wells in the top of the cornmeal mixture.
Crack an egg into each well, if desired, and cover the copper mug with a lid.
